name: Emmett Berry
abstract: Emmett Berry (July 23, 1915 – June 22, 1993) was an American jazz trumpeter. Berry was born in Macon, Georgia, United States.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert with Sarah Vaughan, Erroll Garner, Lester Young and Others

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Moore
about_quotes.evidence: Emmett Berry, George Stevenson, Herbie Hall, Sammy Price, Pops Foster, Fred Moore. A series of recordings made between December 1955, January 1956, and February 1956.

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Thompson
about_quotes.evidence: It was Buck Clayton on trumpet and it was Emmett Berry on trumpet... and there was --let's see on the bass there was -- who was the bass player.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I had Israel Crosby and Emmett Berry -- this was in that era ... and Stan was in the reed section. I had four saxophones.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: the personnel of the band then was ... Russell Smith and Dick Vance and Emmett Berry were the trumpet players.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I was fortunate enough to get Emmett Berry on trumpet... I think that was the band, when I left

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: He had Emmett Berry; he had Sweets, Ed Lewis... This was '46.
